name: West Winterslow
overview: West Winterslow is one of the Winterslow group of settlements (with Middle Winterslow, East Winterslow and The Common) in south-east Wiltshire, about 7 miles north-east of Salisbury and just off the A30 London Road, close to the Hampshire border. It sits on the line of the Roman road and Clarendon Way long-distance path between Old Sarum and Winchester; West and East Winterslow are both recorded in Domesday. The combined Winterslow parish has a population of around 2,000. The Grade I-listed parish church of All Saints at West Winterslow dates from the early 12th century and was rebuilt in coursed flint in 1849-51.
character: A lively, well-established village with a strong community spirit, mixing thatched cottages and period houses with later 20th-century estates, set in rolling chalk countryside.
amenities: Village shop and Post Office (One Stop) in Winterslow
Two pubs: The Lord Nelson at Middle Winterslow and The Lion's Head at The Common
Large modern village hall with recreation rooms, tennis court and events

commutes.notes: About 7 miles via the A30; A303 also close for Andover/Winchester
From Salisbury station, with services also to Southampton and Bath

prices.summary: A broad mix of stock from thatched cottages to modern family homes and bungalows, generally with large gardens and countryside views; a sought-after but mid-priced commuter village. Use the SP5 sold-price record for current context.

thingsToDo: The New Art Centre sculpture park and gallery at Roche Court (near East Winterslow)
Walking the Clarendon Way between Salisbury and Winchester
Bentley Wood nearby for woodland walks and butterflies
pros: Good range of village amenities - shop/Post Office, pubs, primary school and GP surgery
Strong, active community with a large modern village hall
Easy reach of Salisbury plus A30/A303 connections
Excellent walking and woodland on the doorstep
cons: Secondary schooling and main supermarket shopping mean travelling to Salisbury
whoItSuits: Families and Salisbury commuters wanting an active, well-served village within easy reach of the city but firmly in the countryside.
